observations.recommendation:

Prepare reconciliation report on the negative balances of the advances to special disbursing officer and cash local treasury accounts. After identifying the discrepancies, prepare adjusting entries to properly reflect the balances of the accounts;

Utilize the Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due to be submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as basis for setting up the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred Credits at the beginning of the year; and

Prepare a revised SEF Utilization Report that corresponds to the balances reported in the SCBAA through with the coordination of the Municipal Budget Officer. The Utilization Report should serve as a supporting schedule for the balances disclosed in the SCBAA.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has revised the subsidiary ledger for the Trust Fund related to the account 'Due to NGA' to reflect the following adjustments:  

1. Inclusion of downloaded funds from the Department of Agriculture – Region I (DA-R1) amounting to ₱969,766.52 for the retention fee associated with the construction of the canal lining at San Aurelio 1st, Balungao.  

2. Correction of the year-end balance of funds from the SGLG Incentive Fund and the Local Government Support Fund for Financial Assistance (LGSF-FA) due to the erroneous posting of a disbursement of ₱1,680,000.00 from the LGSF-FA to the SGLG Incentive Fund.  

b. The Municipal Accountant is currently analyzing the long-standing negative balances.

The Municipal Accountant made the necessary revisions to the utilization report. During CY 2024, a reconciled report was prepared and submitted to support the Due to NGAs account.

a. The Municipal Health Officer (MHO), Accounting Office, and Treasury Office reconciled their records, and on April 25, 2024, the Municipal Accountant submitted a report detailing the funds received from PhilHealth for CYs 2019 to 2023 to the COA Office, along with photocopies of the issued official receipts.

b. On May 7, 2024, the municipality received a communication letter dated April 29, 2024, from PhilHealth, informing that the Balungao RHU has pending claims due to the non-issuance of official receipts (ORs) or journal entry vouchers (JEVs) for previously credited reimbursements.

c. In response, the municipality promptly prepared a reply letter to PhilHealth, including certified Xerox copies of the ORs (the original copies were submitted to PhilHealth when the fund transfer was recognized), the JEV, and a bank statement for the benefit claims received as of April 2024. Upon submitting the reply letter to the PhilHealth Regional Office (ROI) on May 17, 2024, the municipality also inquired about the process for releasing claims amounting to ₱134,500.00 from calendar years 2019 to 2022. The municipality sought clarification regarding the pending claims, given that the last fund transfer occurred on December 29, 2023, and the corresponding original copies of the ORs had been submitted on time. Additionally, it was noted that the newly appointed MHO has been processing her accreditation with PhilHealth since February 2022. PhilHealth personnel confirmed that the pending claims are still associated with the previous MHO, as they pertain to claims from 2019, and that the stoppage of fund transfers was due to a dormant account. The municipality subsequently requested a bank certification for an active account, which was submitted to PhilHealth on June 28, 2024. On the same day, PhilHealth provided a copy of the Auto Credit Payment Notice for claims covering the period from August 2019 to September 2020, totaling ₱50,350.00. Upon verification with the bank, the total credited amount on June 5, 2024, was confirmed to be ₱50,350.00, and the corresponding ORs were submitted to PhilHealth ROI on July 10, 2024. On July 31, 2024, an additional credited amount of ₱21,500.00 was received, and the municipality continues to coordinate with PhilHealth ROI regarding the period covered by this credited amount.

d. Upon verification of the accounts, the benefit claims with PhilHealth should still be recognized as "Due to GOCC" in the Trust Fund Account due to their special purpose.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:

a) Inform the concerned DILG Office in writing of any adjustments made to the DILG-endorsed GPB or the addition of new PPAs relevant to emerging gender issues or GAD-related activities to ascertain that GAD funds are used on PPAs that address gender issues and uphold gender equality and women’s empowerment in accordance with Item 2, Section 6 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

b) Initiate the reconstitution of the GFPS in accordance with Section 4.1.A.2 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 for a comprehensive identification and response to GAD issues and concerns within the Municipality.

c) Direct the GAD Focal Person to:

i. Maintain an updated GAD Database to facilitate the identification of gender issues in the GAD Plan and Budget.

ii. In coordination with other departments/offices, carefully evaluate the suggested PPAs to ensure they effectively address gender-related issues and refrain from charging expenses to the GAD Fund that are not related in addressing gender-related issues. The municipality may also attribute part or entirely their GAD PPAs, provided it must undergo proper assessment using the HGDG to determine their gender-responsiveness and alignment with the objectives of gender mainstreaming.
observations.managementAction: The Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) assessed the CY 2024 GAD Plan and developed a supplemental plan to comply with the methodologies outlined in the PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars No. 2013-01 and 2016-01. The municipality is committed to accounting for the expenses of Programs, Projects, and Activities in the GAD Accomplishment Report, which addresses gender-related issues.

The municipality committed to ensuring that all bidding documents are secured and complete, and will transmit these documents to the COA Office along with the Program of Work (POW), Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA), and other necessary documentation for audit contract review. Additionally, the Finance Committee, in collaboration with the requesting Office/Department, is dedicated to ensuring the completeness and accuracy of documents prior to processing any payments.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Designated GSO to maintain Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components and regularly reconcile it with the balance per Local Road Network Ledger Card.
Require the Designated GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare a complete lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for the depreciable component of the Road Network at the end of the year.
Prepare a journal entry voucher to take up the proposed adjusting journal entries to correct the understatement of the Depreciation Expenses and Accumulated Depreciation for the previous and current year.
Provide additional disclosure regarding the total Road Network account in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

For the Municipality to have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against the risk of misapplication or misuse, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and materials, except those purchased through PCF;
The concerned offices shall maintain monitoring reports of all receipt and issuances of inventories held for stocks or distributions to the public. These reports shall be submitted periodically to the Municipal Accountant to serve as supporting documents for the timely and appropriate adjustment of inventory balances to the corresponding expense accounts.
The General Service Officer and Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and maintain complete SCs and SLCs for each type of supplies and materials, respectively; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.
The Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical count of all types of inventory items semestrally, prepare the RPCI and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. Also, invite a representative from the COA to act as a witness in the physical count.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the monthly bank reconciliation statements to the bank manager thru the resident auditor for verification of possible errors committed by the bank. Additionally, a copy should also be submitted to the Audit Team for review within 10 days of receiving the bank statement, in compliance with Paragraph 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. This ensures the timely detection of errors or fraudulent transactions, whether committed by the Municipality or the bank and helps maintain the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005 for the proper request for write-off, disposition, and derecognition of dormant accounts.

To safeguard the interests of the Municipal Government against any adverse claims by third parties regarding its properties, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the designated Municipal GSO, to prioritize the application for the transfer and registration of ownership for the nineteen parcels of land with a total book value of ₱76,452,435.00; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds to cover all expenses associated with the land registration process.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to consistently and properly prepare the monthly RAAF to establish the accountability for all accountable forms including checks and facilitate the review and verification of the said report at any given time and enhance controls in fund management. This would enable the Municipal Treasurer to verify that every accountable form is accounted, and balances could be verified with what is actually on hand.
Further, reports to be prepared and submitted must be identified and properly communicated to the person responsible so that no reports will be overlooked.

We have recommended Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Implement comprehensive monitoring controls to ensure timely and accurate remittance of collections by all collecting officers as well as the deputized collecting officers. This should include regular checking to verify that collections are remitted promptly and that the proper procedures, such as completing the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD), are followed at the close of each business day, as prescribed by Section 29 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I;
Set clear remittance deadlines to mitigate delays in the remittance of the deputized collecting officers, such as monthly remittances or whenever a specific threshold amount is reached, subject to the approval of both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Mayor; and
Ensure that all collecting officers, regardless of their appointment status, are required to obtain a fidelity bond in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2019. Further, refrain from any misrepresentation of responsibility, where a collecting officer appears to be in charge of collection, while, in reality, the task is being carried out by a job order personnel.

To mitigate the risk of potential financial losses in case of loss or misapplication of public funds, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to adjust the fidelity bond coverage of the concerned Accountable Officer to align with her highest cash accountabilities. The coverage should reflect the maximum cash collected based on past transactions. Furthermore, Management should implement strict monitoring measures to ensure that collectors’ cash accountabilities do not exceed their prescribed coverage limit.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:
Inform the concerned DILG Office in writing of any adjustments made to the DILG-endorsed GPB or the addition of new PPAs relevant to emerging gender issues or GAD-related activities to ascertain that GAD funds are used on PPAs that address gender issues and uphold gender equality and women’s empowerment in accordance with Item 2, Section 6 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.
Initiate the reconstitution of the GFPS in accordance with Section 4.1.A.2 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 for a comprehensive identification and response to GAD issues and concerns within the Municipality.
Direct the GAD Focal Person to:
Maintain an updated GAD Database to facilitate the identification of gender issues in the GAD Plan and Budget.
In coordination with other departments/offices, carefully evaluate the suggested PPAs to ensure they effectively address gender-related issues and refrain from charging expenses to the GAD Fund that are not related in addressing gender-related issues. The municipality may also attribute part or entirely their GAD PPAs, provided it must undergo proper assessment using the HGDG to determine their gender-responsiveness and alignment with the objectives of gender mainstreaming.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the semi-annual release of barangay RPT shares. Instead, these funds should be remitted quarterly, in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160, to ensure that barangays can utilize them promptly for their operations, projects, programs, and activities. Additionally, refrain from requiring barangays to submit documents as a condition for the release of their RPT shares, as this has no legal basis.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list of taxpayers, showing their names and the amount due and collectible based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card to establish an accurate and reliable basis for setting up of receivables for RPT and SET at the beginning of the year, ensuring the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
The Treasury Office to:
Conduct a sustained information drive and tax campaign to raise the awareness of its constituents regarding the advance payment of the real property taxes and to avail the discounts, encouraging taxpayers to pay promptly and improving the collection efficiency of the Municipality on RPT/SET.
Continue sending letters to delinquent taxpayers and post notice of delinquency at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the local government unit concerned. Also, the Municipality may apply the available legal remedies to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the GSO-Designate and the Inspectorate Team to ensure the proper accomplishment of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports to fully establish accountability; and
Direct the Inspectorate Team to thoroughly inspect and verify the delivered items as to quantity and conformity with specifications based on the delivery receipt and/or approved PO before signing in the “Inspection” portion of AIR.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the POs are properly and fully accomplished to establish their validity and protect the government's interest in case suppliers fail to deliver goods in accordance with the approved PO.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:
Require the end-user units to prepare their respective PPMPs in the prescribed format and properly accomplished for their different PAPs and submit to the Budget Office for evaluation in order to ensure consistency with the Procuring Entity’s ensuing year budget proposal.  The PPMPs shall be forwarded to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into an indicative APP and to the BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement modality.  The APP shall then be submitted to the GPPB as provided in Section 7.3.5 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
Through the BAC Secretariat, to meticulously prepare and finalize the bidding documents in accordance with the appropriate PBD template suited to the nature of each procurement (e.g., Goods or Infrastructure). All documents must be thoroughly reviewed to ensure that there are no blank fields, options, instructional texts (e.g., italicized notes), or sections that are not applicable to the project. Furthermore, the BAC Chairman ensures that all Invitations to Bid included in the bidding documents are properly reviewed and duly signed to validate the authenticity of the procurement process and establish accountability.
Through the BAC Secretariat, to strictly comply with Section 25.7 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 by ensuring the timely issuance of written notification letters to all bidders who submitted their bid proposals, regardless of the number of participating bidders.
Through the BAC Secretariat, to maintain a comprehensive and verifiable record-keeping system, such as a logbook, to document the issuance of Bid Documents. This record should clearly indicate the date of issuance, official receipt number, name of the recipient or bidder, project name, and other relevant details for each transaction.
Strictly require the winning bidder to submit the DOLE-approved CSHP prior to contract signing for all infrastructure projects. Furthermore, it is recommended that the BAC advise and encourage contractors to promptly begin the preparation and submission of the CSHP to the appropriate DOLE Regional or Field Office upon receipt of the NOA, in order to secure early approval and avoid delays in the commencement of construction activities.
Through the BAC Secretariat, to ensure the timely posting of the NOA, NTP, and approved contract on the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period and to attach printed proof of these postings to the contract documents as evidence of compliance.
Through the BAC Secretariat, to ensure that the certifications regarding the advertisement of the ITB and the posting of the NOA and NTP in a conspicuous location on the Municipality's premises are adequately supported with pictures or other evidence. Furthermore, the certifications must specify the exact locations of the postings, the dates they were posted, and be accompanied by time-stamped photographs or other verifiable evidence.
Adopt the latest standard forms and templates issued by the GPPB, including the prescribed Contract Agreement format under GPPB Circular No. 04-2020.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the reclassification of the wages of JO workers, Senior Citizens and Child Development Workers to the proper MOOE account to ensure that expenses are properly presented on the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: page 45 of AAR 2022 | At the time of implementationthe municipal accountant recognized the wages of JO workers. Senior Citizens, and Child Development Workers as Other General Services Expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the following:

a. The Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting journal entries in pursuant to IPSAS No. 1 and to ensure that disbursements are recorded properly for fair presentation of the financial statements; and

b. The Local Finance Committee to thoroughly review the estimated costs reflected in the current operating expenditures for the succeeding budget years to prevent improper charging of expenses to the OMOE account.
observations.managementAction:
page 47 of AAR 2022 | At the time of implementation, the Municipal Accountant refrain from using OMOE account to recognize transactions which can be classified under other specific expense accounts.


The Local Finance Committee reviewed the current operating expenditures in preparation for the annual budget for the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct OIC-Municipal Assessor to reconcile the records pertaining to Land account of the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and (designated) General Services Officer be reconciled with the records of Municipal Assessor in order to ascertain the ownership and valuation thereof. Likewise, exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a TCT in the name of the Municipality pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the officials concerned to undertake and adhere with the COA Circular 2020-006 re: Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of PPE, Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/ Missing PPE Items, for the One Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide impairment loss on the PPE mentioned above in order to correctly present their balances in the financial statements in consonance with IPSAS.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the COA before drawing any journal entry vouchers. Further, to make adequate disclosure on the Notes to Financial Statements for better understanding of item in the Financial Statements.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct MSWDO to prepare and submit list of actual cases handled by the social worker and its duration. Also, the Municipal Accountant to make necessary computation and cause the refund of the overpaid amount, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Reclassify the ₱49,349,802.39 cash outflows pertaining to acquisition of properties and equipment to Cash Flows from Investing Activities for CY 2020; 


Exclude non-cash transactions pertaining to investing and financing activities as reconciling items in the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit); and

Ensure that additional disclosures were included as to the total cash payments for acquisition of long-term assets and that the effects of transactions from Statement of Financial Position to Statement of Cash Flows and even to Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts are presented in a comparable and understandable manner.
observations.reason: Review of the Statement of Cash Flow for CY 2021 revealed that amounts under operating and investing activities in Statement of Cash Flow reconciled with the other Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures and comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS and Manual on NGAS for LGUs by adequately inserting a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant disclosed material changes on the accounts presented in the Financial Statements in its Notes for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to enjoin the Accounting office to exercise utmost care and diligence in recording and classifying the financial transactions to ensure the correct balances of accounts as reflected in the financial statements pursuant to IPSAS 1 which requires fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.reason: Review of the Financial Statements for CY 2021 disclosed that there is no abnormal balance presented in the said Statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to record all inventoriable expenses as Inventory as defined under Section 11 of IPSAS No. 12–Inventories for proper recording in the books of account.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to properly present the adjusted balances of each affected component of net assets/ equity by restating the corresponding prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year Financial Statements in compliance with the requirements of International Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 3.

We recommended and management agreed to provide budget for capital expenditures in the 2021 Annual Investment Program which shall identify the amount of investment in the projects and long-term assets.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer properly classified expenses as to CO and MOOE.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended and management agreed that:

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately send letters to the payees informing them of the existence of these stale checks;

The Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and

The Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with existing rules and regulations particularly on the prohibition on accommodation of personal checks.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant properly adjusted reconciled items, such as stale checks, in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
It is reiterated that Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of effecting payments by issuing checks to creditors without seeing to it that disbursement vouchers pertaining to these payments are completely documents.

It is further reiterated that the lacking documents be demanded to be submitted by those concern in order that the subject “Unrecorded Withdrawal” be properly recorded in the books. In effect a more accurate and reliable financial information.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant vigorously check and review all the supporting documents of each disbursement.
The Municipal Treasurer imposed proper control system to prevent government monies to the risk of misappropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the collector issue the cash tickets in numerical sequence so that balances of unused accountable forms be easily determined.
We recommended and the LGU-Balungao Management agreed to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of Cash Advance and observe those allowable transactions that can only be availed.
observations.reason: Verification of the Status of Outstanding Cash Advances for CY 2021 disclosed that the MLGOO and other officials not qualified to cash advance, other than for travelling, were not granted with CA.

observations.recommendation:
To ensure that financial assistance granted is valid, regular and reasonable, we recommended and Management agreed to: 

Formulate policies and guidelines or adopt the policies and guidelines promulgated by DSWD on the grant of financial assistance under the AICS program to strengthen the controls employed in the selection of eligible beneficiaries; 

Ensure that the MSWDO strictly assess and evaluate all applicants requesting financial assistance out of the AICS program including the identification of nearest kin who serves as the claimant; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to be more circumspect in reviewing completeness of supporting documents.
observations.reason: The Municipality through MSWDO has adopted the policies promulgated by the DSWDO.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the Head of the Procuring Entity agreed the following: 

Direct the BAC Chairman to:
monitor each procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan and reflect the information in the Procurement Monitoring Report as well as the timeline on all procurement activities.
prepare and submit the required Revised Annual Procurement Plan and Procurement Monitoring Report within the prescribed submission in the Government Procurement Reform Act not only to the Department of the Interior and Local Government for the purposes of compliance report for the Seal of Good Local Governance but on the obligations of the agency on the full disclosure and sustainability of transparent practice on how the public funds are managed, disbursed and used.
BAC Secretariat to give utmost priority in the procurement activities in order to expedite the procurement process of the agency as required in the Government Procurement Reform Act.

We recommended and the Management agreed:

To stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.

The BAC to deliberate properly on the appropriate mode of procurement to be used, before the Municipality acquires the needed goods, thus avoiding a similar scenario to happen.

To refrain from indicating in the Purchase Request the specific brand name when purchasing equipment, unless pursuant to the exception stated in Section 18 of the IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Ensure all procurements of the Municipality be conducted through public bidding as mandated under the said Republic Act and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rules XVI of the IRR as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity;

Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted only in highly exceptional cases provided for in Republic Act No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price of the Local Government Unit is obtained; and

Prepare Annual Procurement Plan in the prescribed format of the Government Procurement Policy Board.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Request authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the grant of incentives to municipal employees holding other positions, in accordance with Section 447 (a)(1)(ix) of Republic Act No. 7160; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to check that claims for honorarium are duly supported with the monthly accomplishment reports before effecting payment to ensure that all are proper and valid charges against the funds of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the above-mentioned correcting entry to recognize the asset obtained from utilizing the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities; and

Strictly observe the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards in recording financial transactions.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not received any fund transfer in relation to BGCM for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to check that all disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required documents before effecting payment to ensure that all are proper and valid charges against the funds of the Municipality; and

Submit all the lacking documents of the COVID-19 disbursements as required in Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, Annex “H” Appendix A of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant ensured that all disbursements were supported by complete documentations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the concerned Municipal Office to update the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the personnel in charge of the website maintenance to post the required reports in the official website of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not received any funds in relation to BGCM.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain property, plant and equipment cards duly reconciled with the property cards of the Municipal Treasurer and to invite representative of the Audit Team to witness the annual physical count of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The annual physical count of PPE was conducted on November 2020, and submitted the RCPPE to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Upon receipt of the RCPPE from the Municipal Accountant, the Audit Team evaluated the said report and found that the RCPPE reconciled with the PPE accounts recorded in the books of accounts. And, the PPE accounts were supported with PPE ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
observations.managementAction: The physical count of Local Roads and accomplishment of Road Map were conducted on September 2020.
observations.reason: The Management had submitted the list of Local Roads and Road Map to the Audit Team; however, the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant completely review the identified reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments to effect the reconciling items.
observations.reason: The Cash in Bank-LCCA account presented in the Statement of Financial Position was the same balances in the Bank Reconciliation as of December 31, 2020, hence, all the reconciling items were recorded before preparing the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor/management  agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; 

require all Department Head/ requisitioning units to prepare the RIS for every request for supplies issuances for submission to the Municipal Treasurer/ Acting GSO; and

require the (designated) Supply Officer to prepare the monthly MSSMI duly supported by RIS from end-users for submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant observed the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories. The Department Head/ requisitioning units prepared the RIS for every request for supplies issuances and the designated Supply Officer prepared the monthly MSSMI duly supported by RIS from end-user for the submission to the Accounting Office and COA.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, post-audit of disbursements revealed that the Management implemented the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases over office supplies and drugs and medicines, however, in the amid of pandemic, the Municipality procured various supplies that the Municipal Accountant recorded directly to expense account, as discussed in Finding No. 4.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management expressly agreed and regularly abide that the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the updating of the RPTARs to be able to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and taxes due as basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up of RPT/ SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to update 60% of the List of taxpayers and taxes due.
observations.reason: Verification of records showed that the Municipal Treasurer was not able update the certified list of taxpayers, as the basis of the Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the funds to the General Fund of the Municipality. Further, the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, to comply with the provisions of RA 7160 and for proper presentation of LGU Fund balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Tobacco Excise Tax receipts and disbursements were already maintained under General Fund.
observations.reason: Review of accounting records disclosed that receipts from Excise Tax was already transferred to General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to consider the following audit recommendation:

Maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Section 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445;

Exert diligent effort to locate the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the LGUs recorded obligation and to determine the exact aging of the liability accounts; and 

Balances of fund transfers after the completion of the programs or projects be fully liquidated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still exerting diligent effort to substantiate the LGUs recorded obligation and determining the exact aging of the liability accounts.
observations.reason: Inquiry revealed that the Municipal Accountant is still in the process of restructuring the accounts, and she provided the Audit Team her working paper. Also, she promised to liquidate/ refund the unspent fund transfer received from NGAs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:

To require the Disbursing Officer an explanation on the occurrence of negative balance in Advances for Payroll account ; 

To instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that the cash advance drawn be equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period ; and 

To require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of Advances for Payroll account.
observations.managementAction: The designated Payroll Officer wrote an explanation on the occurrence of negative balance in Advances for Payroll account and the Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entry.
observations.reason: Review of Financial Statements for CY 2020 disclosed that advances for payroll account is already adjusted to reflect the correct balance, but Due to PAG-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts under General Fund, reflected negative balances, as discussed in Finding No. 3.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with approved and completely filled-up Driver’s Trip Tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption before payment is effected, to avoid suspension/ disallowance in audit ;

Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill-up or indicate the following data on the Driver’s Trip Tickets: Time of departure and arrival of the trip, balance in tank at the beginning and end of the trip, distance traveled, odometer reading, if available, liters of gasoline consumed for the trip and purpose of travel for control and monitoring; and

Direct or assign an employee responsible to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis and verification on fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: The Management will strictly comply with the recommendation
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursements for CY 2020 revealed that duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets were attached on the voucher. Also, the Management had submitted the Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations to avoid the incurrence of penalty. Further, the erring Municipal Officials and/or employees return the penalty charged to the fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management will strictly comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The concerned Municipal Officials already refunded the penalty, as evidence by Official Receipt which was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to utilize the scare resources of the Municipality effectively and efficiently. Further, to send officials and/or employees to seminar and training that could truly enhance their skills and/or knowledge in performing their official mandate/ function.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive utilized the scare resources of the Municipality effectively. As a evidence, the Municipality was able to implement infrastructure projects, responded to the needs of the community during the pandemic and other calamities. The Municipality also sustained its operation without incurring loan from bank.

Nevertheless, the Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursement for CY 2020, there is no similar transaction occurred.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and all other concern strictly adhere to the policies/guidelines governing the hiring of Job Orders (JOs) and their entitlements. Management should stop from approving travel order of Job Orders to attend conference/seminars to prevent the LGU’s coffers being used to activities in which it could not benefit.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped from approving travel order of Job Orders to attend seminars/ conference.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursement for CY 2020, there is no similar transaction occurred.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the management agreed to stop the granting of monthly allowance to the Election Officer, and other national government officials, if any, who are not included under Section 2.0 of the Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditure.
observations.managementAction: The Management stopped the granting of monthly allowance to the Election Officer.
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement/ payroll revealed that the Management did not grant allowance to the Election Officer.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the MSWDO prepare and submit the list of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description/ actual duties that describes the basis for justifying such positions who are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to hazardous places as stipulated in DBM and DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, s. 201, and cause the approval of such list by the Local Chief Executive (LCE); and attached to the payroll for hazard pay copy of the DTR and approved leave of absence as certified by the Human Resource Management Officer, if any. Moreover, establish procedures in identifying the personnel assigned to hazardous areas and exposed to occupational risk and duration of their exposure and thereafter it for approval of the LCE.
observations.reason: The MSWDO submitted to the Audit Team, the required documents cited in the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure the grant of overtime services only when extremely necessary and must be supported with proper documents in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2015 on the policies and guidelines on overtime services of government personnel, and Item 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for its basic documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Management stated that claims for overtime were duly supported by necessary documents.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursements for CY 2020 disclosed that overtime services were supported by the required supporting documents as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Accounting Office reconstruct the maintenance/ repair record of each equipment/ vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconstructed the PPE ledger card, which includes the history of repairs of each equipment and motor vehicles.
observations.reason: As per verification, the history of repairs is provided in the PPE ledger cards.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF be included in the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) duly approved by the Local Sanggunian and LCE which shall serve as the basis in the utilization thereof.

Also, the MDRRMC prepare and formulate the disaster risk and reduction management programs/ projects/ activities of the previous years’ balances of LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP. The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities recommended to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) for CY 2014 was reverted back to General Fund. And the unexpended balances of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) for CY 2015-2019 were incorporated in the Revised LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2020.
observations.reason: The Audit Team had verified the Revised LDRRMF Investment Plan, it was noted that CY 2015-2019 unexpended balances of LDRRMF were included in the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the other oversight government bodies pursuant to Section 99 of Republic Act No. 11260.
observations.managementAction: The Management had formulated AAPSI and submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Audit Team had duly received the AAPSI, however, other oversight government bodies was not copy furnished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, together with the accountable officers concerned, to strictly comply with guidelines requiring the timely preparation of reports and submission of accounts. In effect, the establishment of the legitimacy/validity of transactions could also be determined on a timely basis and avoid inconveniencies on the part of those responsible which will include among others the withholding of salaries for their non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The concerned/ accountable employees are exerting effort to meet deadlines and become effective and efficient in performing their duties and responsibilities. Even during the Enhanced Community Quarantine (ECQ), they reported at office in order to update records.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, the Management was not able to submit the accounts within the prescribed period, but we could give due consideration to the Management because there were days when the work is suspended as preventive measures for COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the documents pertaining to the liquidation of the foregoing cash advance to allow the Audit Team to evaluate/ validate the disbursement.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the Liquidation Report and its supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Upon review of the liquidation report submitted by the Management, the documents were in order.

However, for CY 2020, MLGOO was granted cash advance for the payment of various expenses in the conduct of legal forum on katarungang pambarangay, as discussed in Finding No. 12.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to use the prescribed forms presented in COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006 in order to delineate the responsibilities of the Head of the Requesting Office, the Budget Office and Accounting Office as well as the Treasurer in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the use of Disbursement Voucher and Obligation Request forms in prescribed format in COA Circular 2006-002.
observations.reason: Verification of documents revealed that the Municipality used the prescribed format of forms such as DVs, Obligation Request, Purchase Request, Purchase Order, etc.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality pay the taxes due to the national government for the income derived from Mt. Balungao Hot and Cold Spring Resort which is proprietary in nature. Otherwise, the Municipality must present documents to prove that the purpose of its creation, the manner of its operation, intended recipients of its activities and the disposition of revenues are purely governmental in nature.
observations.managementAction:
The Management states in their written reply that the operation of the Mt. Balungao Hot and Cold Spring Resort is purely governmental in nature.

The operation of the Mt. Balungao Hot and Cold Spring Resort serves as one of the municipal enterprises and tourist attraction. It is also included in the assessment area for the Seal of Good Local Governance. The charges and fees that the LGU-Balungao collected from its people and tourists who visit and use the facilities of the resort is merely in minimum amount, to help the general fund to maintain the operation of the said tourism facilities. Thus, the purpose of the creation of the Mt. Balungao Hot and Cold Spring Resort is not proprietary in nature.
observations.reason: The Management submitted the Municipal Revenue Code and Municipal Ordinance No. 2014-004, and per evaluation, the operation of the Mt. Balungao Hot and Cold Spring is not proprietary in nature.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain property, plant and equipment cards duly reconciled with the property cards of the Municipal Treasurer and to invite representative of the Audit Team  to witness the annual physical count of PPE.

We recommended and management agreed that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant completely review  the identified reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

We have recommended  and the  Municipal Mayor/management  agreed to:
require the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I;
require all Department Head/ requisitioning units to prepare the RIS for every request for supplies issuances for submission to the Municipal Treasurer/ Acting GSO; and
require the (designated) Supply Officer to prepare the monthly MSSMI duly supported by RIS from end-users for submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of inventories.

We have recommended and management expressly agreed and regularly abide that the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the updating of the RPTARs to be able to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and taxes due as basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up of RPT/ SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.

We have recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the funds to the General Fund of the Municipality. Further, the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, to comply with the provisions of RA 7160 and for proper presentation of LGU Fund balances in the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to consider the following audit recommendation:
Maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Section 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445 ;
Exert diligent effort to locate the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the LGUs recorded obligation and to determine the exact aging of the liability accounts ; and
Balances of fund transfers after the completion of the programs or projects be fully liquidated
We also recommended that accounts payable which has been outstanding for two years or more may be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the national government provided that there is not actual claim, administrative or judicial has been filed and it is not covered by perfected contracts on record. However, unliquidated balances of accounts payable under the trust funds shall in no case be reverted unless the purposes for which these funds were created have been accomplished.
Ensure that the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for reversion of liability accounts be properly supported with the following:
List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts ; and
Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/ records, financial statements/ schedules and supporting vouchers/ documents cannot be located.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:
To require the Disbursing Officer an explanation on the occurrence of negative balance in Advances for Payroll account ;
To instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that the cash advance drawn be equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period ; and
To require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of Advances for Payroll account.

We have recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are complete before affixing signature in the disbursement voucher. If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, return the DV to the claimant for proper documentation ; and
The Municipal Treasurer pay only claims that are properly approved/ certified and supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We have recommended  and  Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with approved and completely filled-up Driver’s Trip Tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption before payment is effected, to avoid suspension/ disallowance in audit ;
Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill-up or indicate the following data on the Driver’s Trip Tickets: Time of departure and arrival of the trip, balance in tank at the beginning and end of the trip, distance traveled, odometer reading, if available, liters of gasoline consumed for the trip and purpose of travel for control and monitoring; and
Direct or assign an employee responsible to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis and verification on fuel consumption.

We have recommended that Management strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations to avoid the incurrence of penalty. Further, the erring Municipal Officials and/or employees return the penalty charged to the fund of the Municipality

We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to utilize the scare resources of the Municipality effectively and efficiently. Further, to send officials and/or employees to seminar and training that could truly enhance their skills and/or knowledge in performing their official mandate/ function.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and all other concern strictly adhere to the policies/guidelines governing the hiring of Job Orders (JOs) and their entitlements. Management should stop from approving travel order of Job Orders to attend conference/seminars to prevent the LGU’s coffers being used to activities in which it could not benefit.

We have recommended the following:

Create an Inventory Team that will conduct a complete physical inventory of the entire Municipality’s property on a yearly basis. Henceforth, the Designated Supply Officer to maintain updated property cards for all PPEs ; and

Constitute a Disposal Committee to take charge of the property/assets subject for disposal and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for semi-expendable materials and equipment and non-expendable supplies pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions provided in Section 52 of the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring government personnel have enough net take home pay to support for their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.reason: Post-Audit of payroll disclosed that  employees have at least a net take home pay of  P 4,000.00 in compliance with GAA FY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass an ordinance to update the existing Revenue Code and to provide budget for its enactment and publication in order to have a basis in collecting appropriate taxes and revenues to finance priority projects and programs of the municipality.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang  Bayan already passed  an ordinance to update the Local Revenue  Code of the Municipality of Balungao.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and observed the threshold limit subject for Public Bidding provided under the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, the management must refrain from allowing a Job Order to procure items in behalf of the Municipality and eventually subject for reimbursement, given that the purpose they are being hired is only for support functions where no employer-employee exist.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to observe Public Bidding as the primary mode of procurement.  However, they may adopt other alternative modes of procurement such as Negotiated Procurement, with the condition that they must abide with the rules provided in Section 35.5 and 53.1 of the same law. Ultimately, all necessary documents and other requirements be complied and submitted by the officials concerned.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management request the proper write-off and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the above-cited regulations.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transactions of the Municipality before approving the same.  If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, return the DV to the claimant for proper documentation ; and
Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer shall pay only claims that are properly approved /certified and supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, he shall see to it that all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are properly accomplished, and duly approved and/or certified.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management submit copies of POs irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within (5) working days upon issuance of POs ;
Instruct the Supply Officer to notify the Auditor’s Office within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries as per COA Circular No. 96-010 to enable the Auditor to conduct its inspection ; and
Monitor strict compliance to said regulations and enforce sanctions for non-compliance, if necessary.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all Revenue Collecting Personnel to remit their collections at the close of each business day pursuant to provisions of Section 29 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I. Furthermore, collections coming from Real Property Tax must be closely and stringently monitored to avoid late remittance which could possibly result to misuse or loss of public funds.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer designate a separate person to handle the petty cash and payroll fund respectively in compliance with COA Circular 92-382.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Payroll Officer refrain from paying travelling expenses out of the Payroll Fund otherwise it will no longer serve the purpose for which the fund is established.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds. The perforation of the documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but to include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which provide a space for the number of check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces be properly filled up.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe strictly Section 32 of the NGAS Manual particularly on the numbering of supporting documents and the proper listing of these supporting documents on the face of the DV to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the payrolls of regular personnel be prepared and processed on a monthly basis. These payrolls shall be in two (2) sets, reflecting both the 1st and 2nd quicena of the month. The net pay be arrived at by adding the basic salary and all allowances less total deductions divided by two (2).

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer prepare the Monthly Payroll of all the Municipal Officers and Employees in order to unload unrelated work from the Municipal Treasurer and to promote operational efficiency.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management observe properly the audit guidelines in the disposition of government property by inviting a representative from COA prior to disposal/ demolition and not at the time when these assets were already removed or disposed. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary Waste Material Report (WMR) for assets subject for disposal in order to account properly the serviceable scrap materials which still be useful or can be sold to derived additional income from it.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions provided in Section 52 of the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring government personnel have enough net take home pay to support for their daily needs until the next payday.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass an ordinance to update the existing Revenue Code and to provide budget for its enactment and publication in order to have a basis in collecting appropriate taxes and revenues to finance priority projects and programs of the municipality.

Recommendation:
Since taxes are the lifeblood of the government and its prompt and certain availability is an imperious need, we recommend to the management to continue its duty to withhold and remit taxes.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to effect reconciliation of the balances of the asset accounts against relevant records, and to record the appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has conducted its annual physical inventory last September 2016 and the management had reconciled the balances between Accounting Records as against the Per Inventory Records.
observations.reason: The Municipality had conducted their physical inventory and reconciled the PPE to the books of accounts and Report of Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all means to determine the dates of acquisition of the depreciable assets and to compute and record the depreciation immediately in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of providing depreciation expense for all property, plant and equipment of the municipality for CY 2016
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had provided depreciation and impairment loss to PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with Municipal Assessor should prepare Real Property Tax Account Register and submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax / Special Education Tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has no software application yet to generate the actual list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount dues. The Municipal Assessor has agreed to provide Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR), to facilitate the set-up of RPT and SEF Receivables in the books of accounts by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: As per interview with the Municipal Accountant, RPT and SEF Receivables were set-up based on estimates at the beginning of the year, amounting to P3,967,916.25 for RPT Receivables and P3,972,248.60 for SEF Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the dormant accounts and the unsupported balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the final adjustment, when appropriate. Comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to provide further review on the dormant accounts.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 2.B
Upon verification to the Financial Statements submitted to the audit team showed that the dormant accounts with unsupported balances still reflected in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that all necessary documents and other requirements be submitted and complied by the municipal officials concerned.

Further, the Municipal Treasurer pay only claims that are properly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Likewise, see to it that all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are properly accomplished, and duly approved and/or certified.
observations.managementAction: The municipality still failed to check the requirements or supporting documents for each disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Audit Finding No. 3.a There are still Disbursement Vouchers amounting to P 2,268,189.75 were processed even without complete documents necessary to prove the validity, proprietary and legality of the claim.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the above cited provisions in the handling of government funds.  Collections be deposited in full and intact daily or next banking day, to safeguard its funds from the risk of loss or theft and possible misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already scheduled their daily remittances and deposit to the bank, to adhere to the provisions cited after the receipt of AOM issued by the audit team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the audit recommendation cited.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management should be prompt in updating the bond of their accountable officers. Secure the amount of bond required of an accountable officer corresponding to his cash accountability in accordance with the Fidelity Bond Premium Revised Schedule as provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The management had already followed the recommendation cited, by securing the amount of bond required of an accountable officer.
observations.reason: Verification of records revealed that all accountable officers were properly bonded, this was done during the cash examination.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the concerned personnel exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to submit the accounts on time due to various intervening factors such as meetings, requiring the claimant to complete its supporting documents, etc.
observations.reason: Audit Finding No.6 The Municipality was not able to render accounts on time thereby, causing delay in the audit of financial transactions.
